records.applies_when: a feature ships instruction text, docs, or installed blocks that name CLI flags or behavior owned by a later feature
records.lesson: Check installed instruction/doc artifacts against the ACTUAL CLI surface at reconcile - a proved feature's shipped text can advertise a sibling's unbuilt flag, putting doc reality ahead of implementation.

records.applies_when: wiring an init/setup command to a doctor or diagnostic path that can mutate derived state (drain queues, rebuild indexes)
records.lesson: Setup/init verification must be non-mutating - route init through a read-only diagnosis mode; draining/rebuilding belongs to explicit commands, or idempotence criteria silently break as later features extend the diagnostic.

records.applies_when: a success criterion asserts an exact invariant (exactly one commit, exactly N artifacts) over an operation with content- or time-dependent no-op edge cases
records.lesson: Exact-invariant criteria need the no-op edge named and handled - an operation that changes nothing must either report itself as a no-op or be made impossible; tests that sleep past the boundary hide the hole.

records.applies_when: a CLI calls a local model daemon with a client-requested context size (num_ctx) under a strict latency budget
records.lesson: A client num_ctx differing from the daemon's loaded context forces a model RELOAD that dwarfs steady-state latency - first-contact call paths need a budget sized to the cold NFR (or the deployment must pin keep_alive), or the first query after idle silently loses its leg.
